notes: Application development and maintenance is the prime IT outsourcing target per Aberdeen survey; 80% of respondents outsource ADM, with end-user computing (help desk/desktop) a close second at ~79%.
Best in Class organizations save an average of 56% compared to in-house development — more than twice the 26% savings of Industry Average companies.
Industry Average organizations save 26% by outsourcing application development and/or maintenance, compared to Best in Class 56% and Laggard ~2%.
Laggard organizations save very little (approximately 2%) through application development and maintenance outsourcing.
Best in Class organizations achieve 49% increase in user satisfaction through outsourcing; vs. 22% for Industry Average and 5% for Laggards.

metric_value: 67% of projects on or below budget
notes: Best in Class: 67% of projects delivered on or below budget; vs. 51% Industry Average and 26% Laggards.
46% of respondents' companies outsource the majority of their application development work to India; U.S. picks up 30%.
More than half (55%) of companies outsource most of their application maintenance work to Indian providers; U.S. is second at 27%.

metric_value: Slightly more satisfied with India providers
notes: Companies that outsource primarily to Indian providers are slightly more satisfied with quality of work than those that outsource primarily to U.S. providers.

metric_value: 36% cite same time zone proximity as important
notes: 36% of respondents say it's important that provider's team work within or close to the same time zone as the client — indicating popularity of 'near-shore' arrangements.

metric_value: 82% of NA respondents cite cost-cutting as driver
notes: 82% of North American respondents cite cost reduction as chief outsourcing driver — more than four in five — vs. 75% globally; North American firms more sensitive to cost-related issues.

metric_value: 76% say vendor mgmt costs higher than expected
notes: 76% of North American respondents report that vendor management effort and costs are higher than expected.
Top outsourcing driver overall: 75% cite reducing IT operating costs; 63% cite enabling IT to focus on more strategic work.
Second top outsourcing driver: 63% want internal IT to focus more heavily on strategic tasks.
37% of respondents cite gaining access to superior expertise as an outsourcing driver; more important for mid-size firms.
37% cite need for quicker turnaround in application development projects as a key outsourcing driver.

metric_value: 43% cite securing provider with right skills
notes: Top challenge: 43% have difficulty securing a provider with the right skill set; 43% also cite integration/testing/QA taking more effort than expected.
38% cite communication or cultural 'gaps' with the provider as a key outsourcing challenge.

metric_value: 80% of BIC dedicated more management resources
notes: Best in Class response to challenges: 80% dedicated more management time and resources; 80% escalated problems to provider's executive team; vs. ~47-65% Industry Average and 50%/30% Laggards.

metric_value: Mid-market prioritizes expertise over cost savings
notes: Mid-size enterprises more likely to outsource for up-skilling IT staff and improving operations; cost reduction is secondary benefit compared with improved operations and strategic work for IT.
From North America specifically: 45% stays in North America, 42% goes to India, 13% to other Asia.

Research methodology: Aberdeen surveyed nearly 125 enterprises in September-October 2006; ~50% from IT organizations; industries led by high tech/software (30%), finance/banking (13%), transport/logistics (10%).
Survey geography: 39% North America (U.S., Canada, Mexico), 27% Asia-Pacific, 26% Europe, 7% MEA, 1% South/Central America.
Top outsourcing targets: Java and web site development (top), .NET and Windows development, portals development, database and database management.

metric_value: Start small with short-term pilot projects
notes: Aberdeen recommendation for Laggards: outsource small chunks, measure on quality/turnaround/costs vs. pre-established SLAs; start with development before maintenance.

metric_value: BIC: retain strategic work, involve business units in provider management
notes: Aberdeen recommendation for Best in Class: retain portal, open source/Linux, Windows Vista rollout work; involve business units in managing outsourcing providers.

metric_value: Y2K remediation gave India IT industry global foothold
notes: Y2K era (late 1990s) gave India's IT industry foothold: providers took on non-glamorous remediation at fraction of U.S. provider costs; this seeded India's long-term ADM dominance.

metric_value: China, Eastern Europe, Brazil, Canada, Mexico emerging as ADM alternatives
notes: Countries muscling in on India's outsourcing dominance: China, Eastern Europe, Brazil, Canada, Mexico offering more options at competitive cost/quality.

metric_value: Best in Class have cross-functional relationship management team
notes: Best in Class organizations mostly have cross-functional relationship management team to oversee outsourcing relationships; CIO and CTO both play major roles in decisions.

metric_value: Outsource development before maintenance for new practitioners
notes: For organizations with less than 2 years outsourcing experience: outsource development work first and maintenance later — lets organization build relationship management competency with more tangible work product.

Mid-size food & beverage IT manager: when internal developers and outsourced developers work in same area and share techniques, 'the speed at which our staff as well as the outsourced developers learn becomes exponential and it is very cost effective.'
Top benefits from outsourcing ADM: 62% say IT organization could focus on more strategic work; 62% report lower costs; 37% improved documentation; 36% improved version control.

metric_value: Near-shore trend driven by time zone and knowledge transfer needs
notes: Near-shore outsourcing gaining traction: proximity facilitates face-to-face meetings, knowledge transfer during normal business hours — especially valuable for companies outsourcing to gain expertise.

metric_value: Managing outsourced staff requires specialized skills
notes: Industrial equipment manufacturer IT manager: hired two people from large-scale outsourcers to build relationship management team; key skills are customer focus, delivery experience, relationship skills, plus large-scale development and outsourcing project experience.

metric_value: 65% of NA companies sensitive to language/culture gaps
notes: 65% of North American respondents say it's important to have provider whose customer-facing personnel know your language and culture (vs. 49% globally, 38% rest of world).
